<!-- Generated by purlin:spec-from-code. Review and refine. -->
# Anchor: schema_spec_format

> Scope: specs/**/*.md

## What it does

Defines the canonical 3-section format that every Purlin spec must follow. The `sync_status` MCP tool parses specs against this format — violations produce warnings or missing coverage. All skills that create or edit specs (purlin:spec, purlin:build, purlin:spec-from-code) must emit files conforming to this format.

## Rules

- RULE-1: Every spec MUST contain exactly three required sections: `## What it does`, `## Rules`, `## Proof`
- RULE-2: Rules are numbered sequentially as `RULE-N` (RULE-1, RULE-2, ...). Unnumbered lines under `## Rules` trigger a sync_status WARNING
- RULE-3: Proofs are numbered as `PROOF-N (RULE-N)` — each proof references the rule it covers
- RULE-4: Every RULE must have at least one corresponding PROOF
- RULE-5: `> Requires:` metadata, when present, is a comma-separated list of spec or invariant names whose rules also apply to this feature
- RULE-6: `> Scope:` metadata, when present, is a comma-separated list of source file paths the feature touches
- RULE-7: Feature specs use heading `# Feature: <name>`, anchor specs use `# Anchor: <name>`

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): Parse `references/formats/spec_format.md`; verify it documents all three required sections (`## What it does`, `## Rules`, `## Proof`) as mandatory
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): Create a spec with an unnumbered line under `## Rules` (e.g., `- some constraint without RULE-N prefix`); run sync_status; verify it reports a WARNING about unnumbered rules
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): Grep `specs/**/*.md` for lines under `## Proof`; verify every proof line matches the pattern `PROOF-\d+ \(RULE-\d+\)`
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): Create a spec with RULE-1 but no PROOF referencing RULE-1; run sync_status; verify it reports the rule as uncovered @slow
- PROOF-5 (RULE-5): Create a spec with `> Requires: schema_spec_format`; run sync_status; verify the report includes schema_spec_format's rules in coverage @slow
- PROOF-6 (RULE-6): Create a spec with `> Scope: scripts/gate.sh`; verify sync_status parses and displays the scope files @slow
- PROOF-7 (RULE-7): Grep `specs/**/*.md` for first-level headings; verify each starts with `# Feature:` or `# Anchor:`
